item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one doesn't work for me, sorry.

There's far, far too much extra detail in it: in the first paragraph of the containment procedures alone, we're given codes for a procedure and multiple events and a site and even an MTF (and if they're stationed permanently at a site, are they really a mobile task force? Aren't they just personnel?). Neither the containment procedures nor the description are easy to read: it's piles and piles of technobabble and we don't get any context to help us out.

I'm not saying that you need to explain everything immediately. But put some common English sentences in there, if only to break up the measurements and symbols. Redact some of the measurements and tesla values — they don't mean anything for my enjoyment of the piece anyway (70km wingspan? Really? How does nobody notice that? I have a hard time suspending disbelief for that). Same with the Foundation operatives' cover positions: I don't need to know each person was a "Lieutenant Commander, Captain of USS Monaghan (DD-354)" or similar each time. It doesn't add anything to the story.

People talk about a rule of thumb for fantasy books: if you get 1 paragraph in and have already hit 5 made-up words, the book isn't worth reading. That rule isn't always fair or correct, but it does reflect the timeline you're given to suck us in. Drip-feed us the technobabble and give us a few concrete descriptors or procedures, so we're not trying to digest 6 or more unclear terms every few sentences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite satisfying! It's always a treat for me to see real life events woven into a SCP so well. Definitely a +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: A heartfelt thank you to everyone who provided feedback. This was actually the first skip I worked on since joining in 2015. Hopefully it's finally been beaten into a usable article.
